evidence_snippet: Once the Red Wedding begins, the Smalljon defends himself by striking Raymund in the face with a leg of mutton
drinking with his half-brother Lame Lothar, his brother Ser Hosteen

reason: Jinglebell is Raymund's half-nephew — kinship context; the sawing of Jinglebell's neck is Catelyn's action, not Raymund's edge
Catelyn appears as a person Raymund is introduced to — just-mention social setting
Damon appears in a list of drinking companions — co-mention, no direct Raymund–Damon edge
Edmure appears as groom at wedding feast — just-mention setting
The Haighs appear as drinking companions — co-mention group, not a direct relationship
The Smalljon strikes Raymund — no meaningful edge from Raymund's perspective to the Smalljon beyond this incidental contact
Lothar appears as Raymund's half-brother — kinship covered by infobox; just-mention co-presence
Lucias appears as Raymund's goodbrother — kinship context; just-mention drinking companion list
Robb appears as king being stabbed by Bolton — just-mention background event
